steps.title: Find the perimeter of square X
steps.content:
Square X has side length 15 cm. The perimeter of any square is $4 \times \text{side}$.
Perimeter of X = $4 \times 15 = 60$ cm

steps.title: Find the perimeter of square Y
steps.content:
We're told the perimeter of square Y is 3 times the perimeter of square X.
Perimeter of Y = $3 \times 60 = 180$ cm

steps.title: Find the side length of square Y
steps.content:
Since square Y has perimeter 180 cm, and the perimeter of a square equals $4 \times \text{side}$:
$$180 = 4 \times \text{side}$$
$$\text{side} = \frac{180}{4} = 45 \text{ cm}$$
That matches Choice D — $45$.
